reason:
Both answers correctly identify the core rule: the 3-month grace period under §412 closed around mid-April, registration is a prerequisite to suing (Fourth Estate), and pre-registration infringement is limited to actual damages/profits, with practical advice to register now and send DMCA takedowns. Substance is nearly identical between them.

The key difference is how each handles the continuing-infringement nuance. Answer A asserts as settled fact that most courts treat ongoing infringement as commencing at first violation, stated with more certainty than case law actually supports. Answer B presents the same point but explicitly flags it as a circuit split, a more accurate characterization of a genuinely unsettled area, giving the asker a more honest picture without adding much length. B also adds a minor practical touch (sending DMCA notices to search engines like Google in addition to the host).

Both are concise and well organized, with comparable length. B's slightly more accurate handling of the unsettled legal point gives it a small edge.
